jokes.text: To what? - To Criss, Frank.
jokes.setup: Frank asks 'You got married? To what?'
jokes.punchline: Liz matter-of-factly answers as if 'what' is reasonable
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - Frank's dehumanizing question gets a straight answer, highlighting both characters' personalities

jokes.text: What the H-E-double-vibrator is that thing on your left hand?
jokes.explanation: Classic 30 Rock wordplay - juvenile yet clever substitution that's perfectly in Frank's character

jokes.text: How could you do this to me? - Flurb?
jokes.explanation: Liz's nonsense response to Jenna's drama perfectly captures their dynamic - brilliant economy

jokes.text: I guess I ate all that asparagus for nothing.
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cringe comedy - Paul's matter-of-fact disappointment about wasted sexual preparation is perfectly awkward and character-specific

jokes.text: Reverend Gimp
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of inappropriately named reverend
jokes.explanation: Classic 30 Rock throwaway absurdity - perfectly delivered with no explanation needed

jokes.text: Timberbiel, Beyon-Z, Anne Hatha-nobody
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation from standard celebrity portmanteaus to the insulting 'Anne Hatha-nobody' - shows Jenna's cattiness

jokes.text: I was on a list to date Tom Cruise, but I bailed before I got sucked in too deep, praise Xenu!
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy gold - Jenna's casual reference to Scientology as if she narrowly escaped a cult, delivered with perfect obliviousness

jokes.text: It's inspiring to see that a woman in her 30s can still find true love. - I'm 42, Cerie. - I don't know what that is.
jokes.explanation: Perfect Cerie moment - her youth is so extreme she literally cannot process the concept of being 42. Great escalation from insult to existential confusion

jokes.text: 80 Under 80, honoring women in entertainment who aren't Betty White
jokes.punchline: Specifically excluding the immortal Betty White
jokes.explanation: Clever observation about Betty White's seeming immortality in Hollywood - timely and well-crafted

jokes.text: oh, dot com, backslash garbagefile
jokes.explanation: Absurdist tech humor - making fun of convoluted web addresses, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Doo-ba-doo-ba-dow! Ba-doo-pomp-boo-pomp-boo-ba-da!
jokes.explanation: Callback to opening nonsense sounds - shows Liz's consistent awkwardness with expressing joy

jokes.text: She insists on traveling on Pearl Harbor Day to 'Show the Emperor we're not afraid.'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Colleen characterization - her stubborn Irish Catholic worldview manifesting as anti-Japanese sentiment 70 years later

jokes.text: That's only 14 in demon years, Lemon.
jokes.explanation: Brilliant inversion of 'dog years' concept - Jack literally calling his mother demonic while solving a math problem. Perfect economy and originality

jokes.text: I got the idea from watching your President Obama the last four years.
jokes.punchline: Political dig at Obama's perceived inaction
jokes.explanation: Typical Jack political reference - fits character but not particularly original or clever

jokes.text: Notice the ring is shriller.
jokes.punchline: Phone ring somehow reflects her personality
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist observation that technology reflects Colleen's harsh personality - good economy

jokes.text: Well, she's wearing it pulled back, but it's not working. Exactly, because of her ears.
jokes.punchline: Brutal assessment of ear-related hair styling failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Colleen moment - casually cruel assessment delivered through Jonathan. Shows both characters perfectly

jokes.text: Because despite cell phones, iPads, and computers, it's still the most effective portal for poltergeists?
jokes.setup: Kenneth asks why Tracy loves TV
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy logic - completely absurd but delivered with total conviction. Great character integration and originality

jokes.text: Someone's looking lovely today! What a burn! I could have meant someone else. Although I didn't. She's radiant.
jokes.punchline: Treating genuine compliment as insult, then doubling down on sincerity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Tracy logic - he thinks he's being mean but is actually being nice, then realizes it. Perfect character confusion

jokes.text: My whole life is thunder!
jokes.explanation: Episode title and perfect Jenna line - takes metaphor literally and makes it cosmic. Brilliant economy and character integration

jokes.text: Next you'll tell me Mickey Rourke catapulted you into the Hollywood sign!
jokes.explanation: Pure 30 Rock absurdism - completely random but specific imagery that's somehow perfect for Jenna's jealousy

jokes.text: Chuck Scarborough is anatomically a woman? So I am a lesbian.
jokes.explanation: Classic Jenna narcissism - makes fake news about newsman entirely about her own sexuality

jokes.text: What brings a mummer to Lightsman's Row?
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz characterization - theater nerd vocabulary revealing her origins as a lighting board operator

jokes.text: Oh, Liz, you had me at 'Hayden Panettiere is dead'!
jokes.setup: Jenna agreeing to attend award ceremony
jokes.punchline: Mishearing something as celebrity death news
jokes.explanation: Dark and perfect for Jenna - she's excited by what she thinks is a young actress's death. Great misdirection

jokes.text: I'm a nymphomaniac virgin widow, and I just completed my year of mourning.
jokes.explanation: Brilliant logical contradiction - how can someone be both a virgin and a widow? Perfect 30 Rock absurdism

jokes.text: I see her spot in front of my left eye. If this turns out to be a stroke, you're in charge of plucking my chin.
jokes.punchline: Practical grooming concerns during medical emergency
jokes.explanation: Perfect Colleen - even during a potential stroke, she's worried about chin hair. Dark comedy with character specificity

jokes.text: I have a few things I want to say to you before I meet the Grim Reaper, who is black, I assume, what, with the hoodies he wears.
jokes.punchline: Casual racism about mythological figure's clothing
jokes.explanation: Classic Colleen casual bigotry - making death about racial assumptions. Character-specific but uncomfortable

jokes.text: I didn't trust that scout master. He was always wearing shorts.
jokes.setup: Explaining why she faked heart attack
jokes.explanation: Perfect Colleen logic - completely arbitrary moral judgment. Shows her paranoid worldview

jokes.text: If only 'Dragnet' hadn't been preempted because of Sputnik, you never would have been conceived!
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - TV preemption leading to conception. Perfect blend of 1950s culture and family dysfunction

jokes.text: You left crumbs on the floor so that the mice spelled 'Come see me'?
jokes.punchline: Using mice as living text message
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist visual - mice as messaging system shows desperation and creativity

jokes.text: Florence Henderson! - Mrs. Brady! - Uh-uh. I told the black guy here none of that Brady stuff.
jokes.punchline: Real actress rejecting iconic role discussion
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about celebrity typecasting, though not particularly clever or surprising

jokes.text: Are you the perverts who want to go to town on each other while I make a pie?
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to sexual fetish work
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about aging actress taking degrading work - uncomfortable but character-driven

jokes.text: I can't shake hands because I have carpal tunnel syndrome from zipping up my own dress now that Gary's gone.
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny overshare - mundane injury connected to profound loss. Uncomfortable but genuine

jokes.text: Because Gary was the navigator.
jokes.explanation: Running gag about dead husband - building character but getting repetitive

jokes.text: Why does the other Liz Lemon always come up first?
jokes.punchline: Reference to other Liz Lemons online
jokes.explanation: Meta-reference to internet search results and real-world name confusion - mildly clever

jokes.text: Why does the other Liz Lemon always come up first?
jokes.punchline: Implying there's another Liz Lemon who's more prominent online
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating joke about character's lack of prominence

jokes.text: I just went, but I guess I can go again!
jokes.explanation: Mild bathroom humor - low-effort and predictable

jokes.text: You know what my best friend did for my 50th birthday? She gives everybody a car. And then she was all, 'Thank you for coming to Gayle's birthday!'
jokes.punchline: Obvious Oprah reference without naming her
jokes.explanation: Obvious Oprah reference through car giveaways - clever but predictable celebrity joke

jokes.text: My bangs, they're not about you. It's about me trying to cover my forehead.
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about women's relationships and appearance - competent but not special

jokes.text: We're talking about Oprah, right? - No, of course not. She can hear us!
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - treats Oprah like an all-knowing deity. Nice economy and paranoia

jokes.text: For grave robbers.
jokes.explanation: Perfect Colleen - practical paranoia about grave robbers delivered with matter-of-fact tone. Great economy

jokes.text: I went down to a pack a day for you!
jokes.explanation: Classic Colleen logic - reducing from presumably more than a pack a day framed as maternal sacrifice

jokes.text: I was watching TV, and they started interviewing an Asian Santa Claus, and my arm went numb!
jokes.setup: Explaining what happened to her eye
jokes.explanation: Perfect Colleen racism - so shocked by diversity she has stroke symptoms. Character-specific but uncomfortable

jokes.text: My father did not kill dozens of Germans so that his daughter could die in a van!
jokes.punchline: WWII heroics invoked for transportation preference
jokes.explanation: Classic Colleen melodrama - invoking WWII heroics for mundane transportation dignity. Good escalation

jokes.text: The horse is one of only three appropriate subjects for a painting, along with ships with sails and men holding up swords while staring off into the distance.
jokes.explanation: Perfect Colleen rigidity - arbitrary artistic rules delivered with total conviction. Very specific and character-driven

jokes.text: I'm going out of this world exactly the way I came into it. Wearing a hat!
jokes.punchline: Hat obsession as defining life characteristic
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Colleen's fashion rigidity extends to death preparation. Nice escalation of vanity

jokes.text: Are you the same guy as before, or does that sun-bleached turd have a twin brother?
jokes.explanation: Creative Colleen insult - 'sun-bleached turd' is specific and harsh, perfectly in character

jokes.text: Apex Technical School puts students first. And the perfect time to enroll is now.
jokes.explanation: Absurdist Kenneth moment - treating real life like TV with commercial breaks. Moderately clever

jokes.text: Just like Gary and that bitch who was selling us our dream house.
jokes.explanation: Character's grief making everything about her loss

jokes.text: Without me, you'd still be behind that light board in Chicago turning bags of Sour Patch Kids inside out to lick them clean! - I got my money's worth!
jokes.punchline: Liz defending gross candy eating habit
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Liz's desperate past revealed and her pathetic defense. Perfect cringe comedy

jokes.text: I got my money's worth!
jokes.setup: Being accused of pathetic candy behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz response - practical justification for embarrassing behavior

jokes.text: Well, joke's on you because that wasn't a commercial. I don't know what it was!
jokes.punchline: Jenna admits confusion about what she was filming
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna moment - admitting she did something embarrassing without even knowing what it was

jokes.text: Thank you. Visit my website for extras and ringtones.
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna - completely missing the insult and turning it into self-promotion. Brilliant character integration

jokes.text: Well, this stage is now lit like a grocery store milk aisle!
jokes.explanation: Good specific comparison for harsh lighting - professional and relatable metaphor

jokes.text: Also, you need to tell your Aunt Mary, when she was two, there was an accident with a hot corn.
jokes.punchline: Random traumatic family secret involving corn
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist non-sequitur - completely random traumatic family secret. Classic 30 Rock randomness

jokes.text: I breastfed you for nine years!
jokes.setup: Jack telling her not to talk like that
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable escalation - Colleen's inappropriate oversharing about extended breastfeeding. Effective cringe

jokes.text: It's all right! The lack of oxygen is making me orgasm!
jokes.explanation: Dark and uncomfortable - Paul's sexual response to near-suffocation. Perfectly inappropriate for character

jokes.text: You would know, Judy!
jokes.explanation: Mild insult comedy - Bonny lashing out at other women. Not particularly clever

jokes.text: I put his sweater on a body pillow... I took it for a canoe ride!
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of grief behavior - body pillow to canoe trip shows increasing desperation

jokes.text: Colleen had a 'fart attack'?
jokes.punchline: Heart attack autocorrected to fart attack
jokes.explanation: Standard autocorrect joke - predictable technology humor, not particularly clever

jokes.text: My grandmother said, 'Liz, stop playing with the flesh around my elbow.'
jokes.setup: People say strange things at the end
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable specificity - gross but believable deathbed moment. Good cringe factor

jokes.text: Colleen wanted to be buried before the rest of the family found out and sold her body to a haunted house.
jokes.punchline: Family's presumed mercenary attitude toward corpse
jokes.explanation: Dark and specific - Colleen's paranoia about family extending beyond death. Great character consistency

jokes.text: One more chance to disappoint her as she looks up at me from her throne in Hell.
jokes.punchline: Assuming mother is in Hell watching
jokes.explanation: Dark family humor - Jack's certainty about his mother's damnation. Good character relationship

jokes.text: The pill that lets me feel emotion is gonna wear off soon.
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna moment - explaining her rare sincerity as chemical. Brilliant character work

jokes.text: The pill that lets me feel emotion is gonna wear off soon.
jokes.punchline: Revealing emotional sincerity is pharmacologically induced
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna reveal - even her emotional growth is artificial and temporary

jokes.text: They have reinforced crotches.
jokes.explanation: Classic Liz defending her unfeminine choices with practical reasoning. Good character consistency

jokes.text: you're married to a guy that I think about during sex.
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable overshare - perfectly inappropriate for Jenna to admit. Good cringe factor

jokes.text: Pill wearing off. You have mom arms.
jokes.punchline: Immediate return to cruelty as medication ends
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback and escalation - Jenna immediately reverting to type as promised. Brilliant structure and character work

jokes.text: Pill wearing off. You have mom arms.
jokes.explanation: Perfect button to scene - immediate return to shallow Jenna with callback to pill

jokes.text: Where else but real life would a millionaire movie star care so much about a hillbilly janitor that he would spend two days trying to cheer him up?
jokes.setup: Realizing life is better than TV
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment - Kenneth recognizing genuine friendship. Good character development and sincerity

jokes.text: You weren't supposed to have any lines, Quon Lee. Now I have to pay you!
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall about actor payment
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about background actor economics - moderately clever industry humor

jokes.text: I realized that her constant, crushing disapproval was a gift. The greatest gift a mother ever gave a son.
jokes.explanation: Good character insight - Jack finding positive spin on dysfunction. Dark but emotionally true

jokes.text: The man who in 1984 wore a tuxedo so well, he broke up the Go-Go's.
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist achievement - Jack's attractiveness literally destroying a musical group. Perfect ego and specificity

jokes.text: And the plumber says, 'I don't-a know, but that's a pretty big-a pizza!'
jokes.setup: Priest telling inappropriate joke at funeral
jokes.explanation: Deliberately bad funeral humor - stereotypical and inappropriate, which is the point but not particularly clever

jokes.text: I promise to always pour antibiotics all over your penis before you staple it to anything.
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly disturbing wedding vow - implies bizarre sexual practices while showing Jenna's care. Perfectly uncomfortable

jokes.text: I am so honored to be taking your first and last name.
jokes.explanation: Perfect Paul subservience - taking both names shows complete deference to Jenna's ego

jokes.text: Ladies and gentlemen, presenting Mrs. and Mr. Jenna Maroneys!
jokes.punchline: Both taking Jenna's name with plural possessive
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - both become Jenna Maroneys, showing ultimate ego victory. Good callback resolution
